content.text: The system shall allow the user to navigate to the Safety Analytics screen through the navigation path: MHE → MEPS → Safety Analytics, and upon selection the system shall load and display the Safety Analytics dashboard.
navigate to the Safety Analytics screen via MHE → MEPS → Safety Analytics,
I can access safety analytics data.
the user selects MHE → MEPS → Safety Analytics
the system shall load and display the Safety Analytics dashboard.
System shall display a Safety Analytics dashboard page accessible to authorized users.
the system shall display the Safety Analytics dashboard.
System shall display the page title “Safety Analytics” at the top of the dashboard.
the system shall display the title “Safety Analytics”.
System shall display navigation tabs including Driving Safety, Environment Safety, and AI Powered Insights.
I can explore different safety views.
the system shall display Driving Safety, Environment Safety, and AI Powered Insights tabs.
System shall allow the user to switch between Driving Safety, Environment Safety, and AI Powered Insights tabs.
the system shall switch to the selected tab.
System shall display Driving Safety tab as the default active tab when the Safety Analytics page loads.
the system shall set Driving Safety as the default tab.
The system shall display a KPI summary section at the top of the Driving Safety screen containing KPI cards.
I can view key metrics quickly.
the system shall display KPI summary section at the top.
The system shall update KPI values dynamically based on the selected warehouse context.
the system shall refresh KPI values dynamically.
The system shall display the following KPI cards: Safety Violation, Compliance, Skill Score, and High-Risk Session.
the system shall display Safety Violation, Compliance, Skill Score, and High-Risk Session.
Each KPI card shall display a title and corresponding value.
each card shall display title and value.
The system shall support both numeric values (e.g., 5624, 100, 1.52) and text values (e.g., Morning session).
the system shall support numeric and text values.
The system shall calculate and display total safety violations for the last one month by default.
the system shall show total violations for last one month.
The system shall calculate and display compliance percentage on a daily basis.
the system shall display daily compliance percentage.
The system shall update compliance KPI dynamically based on daily operational data.
the system shall refresh compliance KPI.
The system shall calculate and display Skill Score on daily basis.
the system shall display daily skill score.
The system shall display Skill Score as a decimal value (e.g., 1.52) and update on daily basis.
the system shall show decimal value updated daily.
The system shall identify the shift/session with the highest number of safety events and display that session in the High-Risk Session KPI.
the system shall display session with highest safety events.
The system shall update High Risk Session dynamically based on event occurrence data.
the system shall update High-Risk Session KPI.
The system shall display a chart titled “MHE Speed vs Time” at the top of the graph section.
the system shall display the title “MHE Speed vs Time”.
The system shall display a line chart representing MHE speed over time.
I can visualize speed over time.
the system shall display a line chart of MHE speed over time.
The system shall display the X-axis labeled as “Time (per interval)” and plot time in defined intervals (e.g., per 15 minutes).
the system shall label X-axis as “Time (per interval)” with defined intervals.
The system shall display the Y-axis labeled as “Speed (km/h)” representing MHE speed values.
the system shall label Y-axis as “Speed (km/h)”.
The system shall plot multiple MHE data series simultaneously, each represented using distinct colours.
the system shall display multiple lines with distinct colours.
The system shall display MHE identifiers (e.g., MHE-001, MHE-005) corresponding to each plotted line.
I know which line represents which MHE.
the system shall show MHE identifiers for each line.
The system shall display a tooltip on hover over the graph showing time, MHE ID, and speed (km/h).
the system shall display time, MHE ID, and speed.
The system shall provide a Time Range dropdown filter (e.g., Last 15 Min, Last 1 Hour, Last 24 Hours, Last 7 days, Last 30 days).
the system shall show time range dropdown (15 min, 1 hour, etc.).
The system shall provide a Session dropdown filter (e.g., All Sessions, Night Session, Afternoon Session, Morning Session).
the system shall show session dropdown.
The system shall provide an MHE selection dropdown allowing users to select number or list of MHEs (e.g., 5 MHE).
the system shall allow selecting multiple MHEs.
The system shall display the selected MHE count in the dropdown label (e.g., “5 MHE”).
the system shall display selected count (e.g., “5 MHE”).
The system shall display a search input field at the top of the dropdown with placeholder text “Search MHE…”.
the system shall display search field “Search MHE…”.
The system shall display MHEs grouped by category (e.g., Aisle Master, VNA Truck, Stacker, Order Picker, Electric Pallet Jack).
the system shall group MHEs by category.

The system shall display a list of MHEs grouped under their respective operator types (e.g., Aisle Master, VNA Truck, Stacker, Order Picker, Electric Pallet Jack), where each group header represents the operator type, and each item under the group shall display:
the system shall show group headers and MHEs with checkboxes.
The system shall display each MHE with a checkbox for selection and allow users to select or deselect multiple MHEs using checkboxes.
the system shall allow select/deselect multiple MHEs.
The system shall restrict the maximum number of selectable MHEs (e.g., maximum 5).
the system shall restrict further selection.
The system shall display an informational message when the maximum selection limit is reached (e.g., “Select maximum 5 MHE type”).
the system shall show message “Select maximum 5 MHE type”.
The system shall disable additional MHE selections once the maximum limit is reached.
the system shall disable additional selections.
The system shall display the dropdown list in a scrollable panel when the number of MHEs exceeds the visible area.
The system shall update the dropdown label dynamically based on selected MHE count.
the system shall update label dynamically.
The system shall retain previously selected MHEs when reopening the dropdown.
the system shall retain previous selections.
The system shall display a refresh icon/button to reload the graph data.
the system shall reload graph data.
The system shall provide a time range slider below the graph for adjusting the visible time window.
the system shall allow adjusting time window.
The system shall display a chart titled “Operator Speed vs Time”.
the system shall display the title “Operator Speed vs Time”.
The system shall display a line chart representing operator speed over time.
I can visualize operator speed trends.
the system shall display a line chart representing operator speed over time.
The system shall display the X-axis labeled as “Time (per interval)” with time intervals (e.g., per 15 minutes).
the system shall label X-axis as “Time (per interval)” with defined intervals.
The system shall display the Y-axis labeled as “Speed (km/h)” representing operator speed.
the system shall label Y-axis as “Speed (km/h)”.
The system shall plot speed data for multiple operators simultaneously, each represented with a distinct color line.
the system shall display multiple lines with distinct colours.
The system shall associate each plotted line with an operator name (e.g., Kunal Deshmukh, Vikram Deshmukh, Abhishek Sharma).
the system shall associate each line with operator name.
The system shall display a tooltip on hover showing: time, operator name, and corresponding speed value with unit (km/h).
the system shall display time, operator name, and speed (km/h).
The system shall provide a Time Range dropdown filter (e.g., Last 15 min, Last 1 Hour, Last 24 Hours, Last 7 days, Last 30 days).
the system shall display time range options.
The system shall provide a Session dropdown filter (e.g., All Sessions, Night Session, Afternoon session, Morning session).
the system shall display session options.
The system shall provide an Operator selection dropdown (e.g., 4 Operators) to allow selection of multiple operators.
the system shall allow multi-operator selection.
The system shall display the count of selected operators in the dropdown label (e.g., “5 Operators”).
I know how many operators are selected.
the system shall display selected count.
The system shall display a search input field with placeholder text “Search operator…” within the dropdown.
the system shall show search input “Search operator…”.
The system shall allow users to search operators by operator name and dynamically filter the operator list based on the entered search input.
the system shall filter operator list dynamically.
The system shall display a list of operators, where each operator entry includes: - Checkbox for selection - Operator name - Operator role (e.g., Operator, Warehouse Equipment Operator)
each operator shall display checkbox, name, and role.
The system shall allow users to select and deselect operators using checkboxes.
The system shall display the selection count indicator inside the dropdown (e.g., “5 of 5 selected”).
the system shall show selection count (e.g., “5 of 5 selected”).
The system shall restrict the maximum number of selectable operators (e.g., maximum 5 operators).
The system shall display an informational message when the selection limit is reached (e.g., “Select maximum 5 operators”).
The system shall display the operator list in a scrollable panel when the number of operators exceeds the visible area.
The system shall apply selected operators as a filter to update the graph data dynamically.
The system shall close the dropdown when the user clicks outside the dropdown area.
The system shall display a refresh icon/button to reload the graph data.
The system shall display a highlighted time region on the graph representing the selected or focused time window.
system shall highlight selected time region.
The system shall provide a time range slider below the graph to adjust the visible time range.
system shall allow time range adjustment.
The system shall allow users to drag slider handles to zoom in or out of specific time intervals.
system shall adjust time intervals dynamically.
The system shall handle scenarios where no operator data is available by displaying a message such as “No data available”.
The system shall display a section titled “Skill vs Behaviour Quadrant”.
see the Skill vs Behaviour section,
the system shall display the title “Skill vs Behaviour Quadrant”.
The system shall display a scatter plot graph representing operator Skill vs Behaviour.
I can visualize Skill vs Behaviour.
the system shall display a scatter plot graph.
The system shall plot each operator as a data point based on Skill (X-axis) and Behaviour (Y-axis).
the system shall plot each operator based on Skill and Behaviour.
The system shall display the X-axis labeled as Skill and Y-axis labeled as Behaviour.
the system shall label X-axis as Skill and Y-axis as Behaviour.
The system shall divide the graph into four quadrants using horizontal and vertical reference lines.
the system shall divide the graph into four quadrants.
The system shall display quadrant labels indicating performance zones (e.g., High Skill, Low Skill, Good Behaviour, Bad Behaviour).
the system shall display labels like High Skill, Low Skill, Good Behaviour, Bad Behaviour.
The system shall display operator data points using visual indicators (dots/bubbles).
the system shall represent operators using dots or bubbles.
The system shall calculate Skill Score based on operator performance and behavioural events.
the system shall calculate Skill Score based on performance and behaviour.
The system shall update the Skill vs Behaviour data on a daily basis.
the system shall update Skill vs Behaviour data.
The system shall provide a refresh icon/button to manually reload the graph data.
the system shall reload graph data.
The system shall display updated data after refresh action.
the system shall display updated graph data.
The system shall display a tooltip when the user hovers over a data point, containing the operator’s name along with corresponding Skill Score and Behaviour Score values.
user hovers on a data point
the system shall display operator name, Skill Score, and Behaviour Score.
The system shall display a section titled “Detailed Driving Sessions”.
the system shall display “Detailed Driving Sessions”.
The system shall display a subtitle describing the section (e.g., “Complete overview of current continuous driving sessions”).
the system shall show subtitle text.
The system shall display a list of currently active operators working in the warehouse.
the system shall display active operators.
The system shall display the following columns for each operator: - Operator Name - MHE ID - Start Time - Duration - Last Break Time - Status
the system shall display Operator Name, MHE ID, Start Time, Duration, Last Break Time, and Status.
The system shall calculate and display Duration based on the difference between current time and session start time.
the system shall calculate duration from start time to current time.
The system shall display Last Break Time for each operator.
the system shall show Last Break Time.
The system shall display Status using color-coded badges (e.g., Normal in green).
the system shall use color-coded badges.
The system shall display only currently active/working operators in the list.
the system shall show only active operators.
The system shall update the list dynamically based on real-time operator activity.
the system shall update list dynamically.
The system shall display a policy limit indicator (e.g., “Policy Limit – 180 min”).
the system shall display policy limit (e.g., 180 min).
The system shall provide a refresh icon/button to reload session data manually.
the system shall reload session data.
The system shall support pagination controls (e.g., rows per page, next/previous navigation).
the system shall provide pagination controls.
the system shall update rows per page.
The system shall handle empty state by displaying a message such as “No active sessions available”.
the system shall display “No active sessions available”.
The system shall display a section titled “Operator Accountability Report”.
see the Operator Accountability Report section,
the system shall display the title “Operator Accountability Report”.
The system shall display a subtitle describing the report (e.g., “Overspeeding, fatigue, rash driving, and safety compliance by shift”).
the system shall show the subtitle describing the report.
The system shall display a tabular report of operators with safety and behavioural metrics.
the system shall display a table of operator metrics.
The system shall display the following columns in the table: - MHE Operator - Overspeed Count - Fatigue Alerts - Rash Driving - Location Compliance - Safety Score
the system shall display columns: MHE Operator, Overspeed Count, Fatigue Alerts, Rash Driving, Location Compliance, Safety Score.
The system shall display operator name along with avatar initials (e.g., AM, SK, KD) in the MHE Operator column.
the system shall display operator name with avatar initials.
The system shall display Overspeed Count representing the number of Overspeeding events per operator.
the system shall display overspeed count per operator.
The system shall display Fatigue Alerts representing fatigue-related events per operator.
the system shall display fatigue alerts per operator.
The system shall display Rash Driving count representing unsafe driving events per operator.
the system shall display rash driving count per operator.
The system shall display Location Compliance count representing violations related to restricted or incorrect locations.
the system shall display location compliance count.
The system shall calculate and display a Safety Score (%) for each operator based on defined safety parameters.
the system shall calculate Safety Score (%).

104.title: Display Safety Score as Progress Bar

content.text: The system shall display Safety Score using a progress bar with percentage value.
the system shall show it as a progress bar with percentage.
The system shall visually represent Safety Score using color-coded indicators (e.g., green for high score, yellow for medium, grey/low for poor score).
the system shall use color-coded indicators (green, yellow, grey).
The system shall display Safety Score values in percentage format (e.g., 75%, 87.51%).
the system shall display values in percentage format.
The system shall update the report data based on selected shift or session context.
the system shall update report based on selected session.
The system shall provide a refresh icon/button to reload the report data.
the system shall reload report data.
The system shall update the report dynamically based on the latest available data.
the system shall update report dynamically.
The system shall handle cases where no data is available by displaying an appropriate message (e.g., “No data available”).
the system shall display “No data available”.
The system shall support vertical scrolling if the number of operators exceeds the visible table area.
the system shall support vertical scrolling.
The system shall support pagination controls (e.g., rows per page, next/previous navigation).
the system shall provide pagination controls.
The system shall allow users to select rows per page (e.g., 10).
the system shall update the table accordingly.
The system shall display total records count (e.g., “1–1 of 10”).
the system shall display total record count (e.g., “1–1 of 10”).
